title: LYRA Audit Output v1.1
description: Schema for all LYRA audit agent and synthesizer outputs. Every run must produce JSON conforming to this schema. v1.1 adds: kind field, typed proof hooks, coverage declaration, structured ranked_plan, preflight support.

schema_version.description: Schema version. Breaking changes increment major, additions increment minor.

kind.description: Distinguishes raw agent output from merged synthesizer output. Downstream tooling can branch on this without parsing agent/suite fields.

run_id.description: Unique ID for this run. Format: <suite>-<YYYYMMDD>-<HHmmss>. Example: logic-20250304-143022

branch.description: Git branch name. Use 'unknown' if not in a git repo.

commit.description: Git commit SHA (short or full). Use 'unknown' if unavailable.

tool_platform.description: Platform used. Examples: cursor, github-copilot, claude-code, manual

model.description: Model identifier. Examples: claude-sonnet-4-5, gpt-4o, gemini-2.0-flash

preflight_artifacts.description: Optional. Paths to baseline captures taken before agents ran. Agents should reference these for evidence.

test_output.description: Path to test runner output, e.g. audits/artifacts/_run_/tests.txt

notes.description: Optional free-text context about this run.

suite.description: Suite name. One of: logic, data, ux, performance, security, deploy, synthesized

name.description: Agent identifier. Examples: runtime-bug-hunter, schema-auditor, ux-flow-auditor, synthesizer

role.description: One-sentence description of what this agent does.

inputs_used.description: Files, commands, logs, or artifacts the agent actually examined.

stop_conditions_hit.description: Any stop conditions triggered during this run.

coverage.description: What the agent examined and what it skipped. Helps the Synthesizer decide if re-audit is needed.

files_examined.description: Repo-relative paths or glob patterns the agent inspected.

files_skipped.description: Paths skipped and why (e.g., 'dist/** - generated code').

coverage_complete.description: True if the agent exhausted its scope. False if it stopped early (hit finding limit, stop condition, or context window).

incomplete_reason.description: Required when coverage_complete is false. Why the agent stopped early.

by_status.description: Distribution by lifecycle status. Example: {open: 5, in_progress: 2, fixed_verified: 3}

next_actions.description: Top 3-5 recommended next actions from this agent.

diff_summary.description: Synthesizer output only. Summary of changes vs prior open_findings.json.

compared_against.description: Run ID or path of the prior state compared against. Use 'none' for first run.

ranked_plan.description: Synthesizer output only. Structured execution plan.

top_fixes.description: Top 10 fixes ranked by severity, priority, confidence, then effort (smallest first).

commit_plan.description: Commit-sized patch steps for the top fixes.

regression_checklist.description: Concrete checks to run after applying patches. Tied to touched surfaces.

scope.description: Which files or surfaces to focus on.

reaudit_plan.description: Targeted re-audit assignments. Only affected agents and surfaces.

finding_id.description: Stable deterministic ID. Preferred: 'f-' + first 8 hex chars of SHA-256(type|category|file_path|symbol|title). Fallback: f-<category_slug>-<file_slug>-<counter>.

type.description: bug = broken behavior. enhancement = missing capability. debt = working but fragile/costly. question = ambiguous, needs human decision.

category.description: Agent-specific category. Examples: null-ref, missing-rls, copy-mismatch, n-plus-one, xss, missing-error-boundary, auth-bypass, bundle-size

severity.description: Harm and correctness risk. Blocker = app broken or data loss. Major = significant degraded experience. Minor = cosmetic or edge case. Nit = style or preference.

priority.description: Sequencing and value. P0 = fix before next deploy. P1 = fix this sprint. P2 = fix this cycle. P3 = backlog.

confidence.description: evidence = directly observed/reproduced. inference = logically deduced from code/config. speculation = pattern-based guess, must include verification steps.

description.description: Full explanation. Include what is wrong, why it matters, and what triggers it.

proof_hooks.description: At least one proof hook is required. These anchor the finding to specific code, runtime output, or UI behavior.

repro_steps.description: Ordered steps to reproduce the issue. Optional for enhancements and debt.

impact.description: What happens if this is not fixed. Be specific: user-visible effect, data risk, cost risk.

approach.description: Brief fix strategy. Prefer minimal diffs.

estimated_effort.description: trivial = <15min. small = <1hr. medium = <4hr. large = <1day. epic = multi-day.

risk_notes.description: What could go wrong with this fix. Regressions, side effects.

tests_needed.description: Specific tests to write or run to verify the fix.

owner.description: Who is responsible. Default solo-dev for solo projects.

related_ids.description: Finding IDs that are duplicates, dependencies, or share root cause.

history.description: Timeline of events. Must contain at least one 'created' event.

artifacts.description: Repo-relative paths under audits/artifacts/<FINDING_ID>/. Logs, screenshots, traces, profiles.

ProofHook.description: Typed proof hook. Each hook_type carries specific optional fields. The summary field is always required as a human-readable one-liner.

hook_type.description: code_ref = file+symbol+lines. error_text = runtime error. command = CLI invocation. repro_steps = numbered steps. ui_path = route+interaction. data_shape = expected vs observed schema. log_line = log excerpt. config_key = env/config. query = SQL/DB. artifact_ref = path to captured file.

summary.description: Human-readable one-liner explaining what this hook proves.

file.description: Repo-relative file path. Used by: code_ref, log_line, config_key.

symbol.description: Function, component, or class name. Used by: code_ref.

start_line.description: Starting line number. Used by: code_ref.

end_line.description: Ending line number. Used by: code_ref.

error_text.description: Exact error message. Used by: error_text.

command.description: CLI command to run. Used by: command.

expected.description: Expected output. Used by: command, data_shape.

actual.description: Actual output. Used by: command, data_shape.

steps.description: Numbered steps. Used by: repro_steps, ui_path.

route.description: URL path or route name. Used by: ui_path.

selector.description: CSS selector or component name. Used by: ui_path.

observed_schema.description: What the data actually looks like. Used by: data_shape.

expected_schema.description: What the data should look like. Used by: data_shape.

config_key.description: Configuration key name. Used by: config_key.

config_value.description: Configuration value (redact secrets). Used by: config_key.

query_text.description: SQL or database query. Used by: query.

artifact_path.description: Repo-relative path to an artifact file. Used by: artifact_ref, and optionally by any hook type.

actor.description: Who or what created this event. Examples: runtime-bug-hunter, solo-dev, synthesizer

artifacts.description: Paths to artifacts created during this event.

pr.description: Pull request URL or number if relevant.

why_now.description: Why this fix is ranked here. Impact, risk, and effort reasoning.

risk.description: What could go wrong if we fix this (or do not).

CommitPlan.description: One commit-sized unit of work. May address one or more related findings.

title.description: Git commit message for this unit of work.

finding_ids.description: Finding IDs addressed by this commit.

steps.description: Ordered steps to implement the fix.

tests_or_checks.description: Tests to write or run to verify this commit.
